topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

I was halfway through drafting a deeply personal message to my best friend, a rambling anecdote about my weekend, when my thumb slipped. I meant to type 'Sarah,' but instead, the screen displayed a name I didn't recognize. A jolt, sharp and immediate, shot up my arm. I froze, my breath catching in my throat. My eyes darted from the phone screen to the recipient field, then back again, a slow, creeping realization dawning. It was a text addressed to Mr. Henderson, my notoriously stern, distant boss. My mouth went dry. I quickly swiped to delete, but the words were already sent. A cold sweat broke out on my palms. I stared at the sent message, a strange, wide-eyed stillness settling over me as I waited for the inevitable fallout.

[story 2]

The notification pinged, and my heart gave a sudden, frantic thump against my ribs. I had intended that message for my sibling, a simple check-in about dinner plans. Instead, the screen glowed with a message clearly aimed at a stranger—a very formal-sounding inquiry about a rare antique clock. I blinked several times, my focus completely shattered. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. My jaw dropped slightly, and I let out a small, involuntary puff of air through my nose. Who on earth was Mr. Abernathy? I frantically pulled up the messaging app, my fingers trembling as I tried to backtrack, but the digital trail was already set. A profound sense of disorientation washed over me; the mundane reality of the moment had just fractured.

[story 3]

It happened so fast. One second I was composing a silly meme for my group chat, the next, my finger landed on the wrong contact. The recipient field flashed, and a sickening lurch occurred in my stomach. I stared at the screen, my pupils dilating as I tried to process the absurdity of the situation. My hands instinctively flew to my chest, pressing down as if to physically contain the sudden surge of disbelief. A slow, shaky exhale escaped my lips. This wasn't just a typo; this was… something else entirely. I stood up abruptly, pacing two steps before stopping dead, my gaze fixed on the glowing rectangle, completely unable to move or speak.
